entities.name: cost model at the job-application step
entities.context: Clarified that there is distinct incremental cost per job application

entities.name: incremental cost
entities.context: Cost each time a candidate applies, even with prior resume/story analysis

entities.name: dense alignment
entities.context: Process of aligning ~30 role items with candidate stories and resume

entities.name: vibe check
entities.context: Comparatively cheap screening mechanism to gate expensive analysis

entities.name: front-door filter
entities.context: Gating mechanism using vibe checks before high-cost analysis

entities.name: vibe-check threshold
entities.context: Minimum score required for candidate to proceed to expensive analysis

entities.name: Do not flush money UI principle
entities.context: Design principle to avoid UI patterns that trigger heavy analysis for low-signal candidates

relationships.to: cost model at the job-application step
relationships.context: Confirmed the existence of incremental costs per job application

relationships.from: cost model at the job-application step
relationships.context: The cost model centers on incremental costs per application
Cost comes from decomposing roles and running dense alignment
Agreed vibe checks should gate access to expensive analysis
Vibe checks serve as the required front-door filter
Threshold determines which candidates pass the vibe check

relationships.to: Do not flush money UI principle
relationships.context: Identified UI patterns that waste money and established this as a principle
